claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 2 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 3 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children I'd like to ask about - Alex who is 3, Blake who is 2, and Charlie who is also 3.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children - Alex (age 3), Blake (age 2), and Charlie (age 3).

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of them, I need to ask: Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to understand your living arrangements with the children.

Do all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) currently live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, only Blake lives with me. Alex and Charlie don't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. So Blake lives with you, but Alex and Charlie don't.

For Alex and Charlie who don't live with you - are you paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit would be (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them? This could include contributions for things like food, clothes, or pocket money.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards their upkeep. I help with things like clothes and other expenses they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful to know. To better understand your contributions for Alex and Charlie, can you tell me approximately how much you contribute towards each of them per week or per month? 

I ask because to qualify for Child Benefit when a child doesn't live with you, you need to contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment itself.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I contribute about £26 per week for Alex. For Charlie, their other parent who lives with them already claims Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that detail. That's very helpful.

Just to confirm I have the complete picture:
- Blake (age 2) lives with you
- Alex (age 3) doesn't live with you, but you contribute about £26 per week towards their upkeep
- Charlie (age 3) doesn't live with you, and their other parent already claims Child Benefit for them

Is this all correct? And are there any other circumstances I should know about, such as any of the children being in hospital, care, or any other special situations?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's all correct. And no, there are no other special circumstances - none of them are in hospital or care or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, here's my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility for each of your children:

**Alex (age 3): You ARE eligible ✓**
Even though Alex doesn't live with you, you're contributing approximately £26 per week towards their upkeep. Since this meets or exceeds the Child Benefit amount, you qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Blake (age 2): You ARE eligible ✓**
Blake lives with you in the UK and is under 16, so you meet the standard criteria for Child Benefit.

**Charlie (age 3): You are NOT eligible ✗**
Unfortunately, since Charlie's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for them, you cannot also claim it. Only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child.

**Summary:** You can claim Child Benefit for Alex and Blake, but not for Charlie.

If you'd like to proceed with claiming, or if you have any questions about the High Income Child Benefit tax charge (which applies if you or your partner earn over £60,000), please let me know!

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old (under 16) and does not live with the claimant. However, the claimant contributes approximately £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep (for clothes and expenses). According to the rules, you can qualify for Child Benefit if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after the child. The current Child Benefit rate for one child is approximately £25.60 per week, so the claimant's contribution of £26 per week meets this threshold. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 2 years old (under 16) and lives with the claimant in the UK. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 3 years old (under 16) and does not live with the claimant. According to the rules, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child. The claimant has stated that Charlie's other parent, who lives with Charlie, already claims Child Benefit for them. Since another person is already claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, the claimant cannot also claim it. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children: Alex (3), Blake (2), and Charlie (3). The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Blake who lives with them, and for Alex because they contribute at least the equivalent of Child Benefit (£26/week) towards Alex's upkeep even though Alex doesn't live with them. However, the claimant is not eligible for Charlie because another person (Charlie's other parent) is already claiming Child Benefit for that child, and only one person can claim Child Benefit per child.
